judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports correctly and thoroughly satisfy all four expectations; they are comparable, with Report 2 adding minor extra detail (normalize divergence, git-log confirmation).

report_1.evidence: `config/scoring_config.yaml` still says `expected_label_field: label`, but every record in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` now stores the gold label under the key `gold_label` (not `label`). Because `.get()` is used instead of direct indexing, the missing key doesn't raise — it silently returns `None`, so `prediction == gold` is `False` for every single record ... The file that needs to change is **`ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`**

report_2.evidence: That file still has `expected_label_field: label`, but the eval fixture (`fixtures/eval_set.jsonl`) was migrated to use the key `gold_label` ... `record.get(gold_field)` returns `None` for every single record ... **The fix is to change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label` in `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`.**

report_1.evidence: This matches commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)", authored 2026-06-17 13:00:00 +0000), which renamed the gold-label key in every record of `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` from `label` to `gold_label` and updated `score_batch.py`'s `REQUIRED_KEYS` set accordingly — but did not update `config/scoring_config.yaml`'s `expected_label_field`

report_2.evidence: This lines up exactly with commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)", authored 2026-06-17 13:00 UTC), which renamed every record's label key from `label` to `gold_label` in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` ... but did not update the separate `expected_label_field: label` setting in `config/scoring_config.yaml`

report_1.evidence: That script never reads `config/scoring_config.yaml` or the gold field at all (`score_single` just calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and returns the prediction) — it cannot exercise the bug regardless of how many times it's run ... Correct reproduction: run the nightly batch job itself (`python3 -m pipeline.score_batch` ... not a single-item helper script.

report_2.evidence: `pipeline/score_one.py` is a single-item helper that only calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and prints the predicted category — it never loads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never looks up a gold label, and never does any comparison at all ... it structurally cannot exercise the config/eval-set field mismatch ... To correctly reproduce: run the actual nightly command (`python3 -m pipeline.score_batch` ...

report_1.evidence: This plan should not be acted on — it targets a non-existent cause and would not fix anything ... There is no "model" to switch: the classifier is a deterministic keyword-rule stub ... It also leaves the actual defect — silent `.get()` masking of missing/misnamed fields in the scorer — completely unaddressed

report_2.evidence: This plan should not be acted on — it targets the wrong hypothesis and would not fix or even diagnose anything ... there is no model to switch back to and no model-side change occurred ... would not change the accuracy, since the bug is a pure config/data-schema mismatch in the scoring comparison, unrelated to which model or classifier version is used.
